title: aristolochia oblongata subsp. maestrensis
intro: Aristolochia oblongata subsp. maestrensis, also known as the Maestrensis birthwort, is a plant species belonging to the Aristolochiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots and stems, which contain aristolochic acid, a compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The Maestrensis birthwort is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, although its effectiveness and safety are still being studied. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are used in folk medicine, although their use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
